candidates.name: Na0.35CoO2.H
candidates.notes: H replaces H2O between CoO2 layers. Higher omega_ph from lighter H but limited lambda_ph because Co-H coupling is indirect (H sits between layers). Even with mu*=0, total coupling is too weak for high Tc.

candidates.name: Cd2Re2O7.H
candidates.notes: Cd2Re2O7 already has very low lambda_sf, but this is because correlations are weak (5d system), NOT because frustration selectively suppressed SF. With s-wave pairing and mu*=0.10, Tc ceiling is limited. E_hull > 50 meV/atom: likely UNSTABLE.

candidates.name: Nd2Ir2O7.H
candidates.notes: Insulating base material; would need heavy doping to metallize. Even if metallic, s-wave pairing gives mu*=0.10. E_hull > 50 meV/atom: likely UNSTABLE.

candidates.name: kappa-BEDT-Br.H (hypothetical)
candidates.notes: HYPOTHETICAL ONLY. Organic crystal structure cannot incorporate atomic H. Included for completeness to show even the best-case frustrated triangular system would not reach 300 K.

candidates.name: HxCoO2 (direct H in layer)
candidates.notes: Hydrogen bronze analog of cobaltate. H bonds to apical O, modifying Co-O hybridization. Better lambda_ph than intercalated version but still limited by weak Co-H indirect coupling.
